card.name: ALLEYWAY
card.description: As if the townsfolk had fled in great haste, broken crates on the ground and shattered cobblestones lead you into an alleyway.
card.rules: A Corridor will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event occurs during the Power Phase.

card.name: BACK GARDEN
card.description: A garden rest area seems to offer the group a brief moment of respite.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: BROKEN ROAD
card.description: There is a pit in the middle of the road, though someone has laid some old planks across it to pass.
card.rules: A Corridor will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event occurs during the Power Phase.

card.name: CORNER
card.description: The streets of the city turn at your path. No one remains, yet everything is full of closed coffins.
card.rules: A Corridor will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event occurs during the Power Phase.

card.name: EAST DISTRICT
card.description: In this district there is a bloodstained column in the centre, and a large crate.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: ENTRANCE
card.description: An access point to the city that serves as a crossroads of streets. So much silence is deeply unsettling.
card.rules: A Corridor will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event occurs during the Power Phase.

card.name: GRAVEYARD
card.description: The city's necropolis, where all the townsfolk and villagers who died were laid to rest.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: GREEN ZONE
card.description: A leafy tree fills the room, offering some shade all around it. Ideal for a brief pause.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: MONUMENT
card.description: In the centre of the plaza, an immense monument rises. It seems especially strange when the moonlight reflects on it.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: OLD TOWN
card.description: The city centre lies abandoned. As you arrive, you sense a kind of magical aura surrounding the place. Everything is in ruins after the siege.
card.rules: For details about this room, consult the Adventure Book.

card.name: PIT OF CORPSES
card.description: There is a small water fountain beside a nauseating smell coming from a pit filled with skulls and bones.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: SEWER GRATE
card.description: Beneath the great grate, water runs, falls, and splashes everywhere. It might be an escape route.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: SHORT STREET
card.description: A narrow, short street where the cobblestones have been worn down by the passage of time.
card.rules: A Corridor will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event occurs during the Power Phase.

card.name: SHORT STREET II
card.description: The city's sewer system bears an engraved emblem you do not recognize at all.
card.rules: A Corridor will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event occurs during the Power Phase.

card.name: STREET CROSSING
card.description: An intersection where the streets continue endlessly between tall ruined buildings. The city has a grim, gothic character.
card.rules: A Corridor will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event occurs during the Power Phase.

card.name: TOPPLED STATUE
card.description: A gigantic stone idol has been cast down and shattered into hundreds of pieces.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: WILL-O'-THE-WISPS
card.description: At this spot, four ghostly lights illuminate the citadel with an intense blue glow.
card.rules: A Corridor will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event occurs during the Power Phase.

card.name: WISHING WELL
card.description: The bottom of the well cannot be seen, and even shouting into it brings back an echo. There are traces of fresh blood near the dark well.
card.rules: Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: BOTTOMLESS PIT
card.description: Hovering over a bottomless pit is a series of wooden planks tied precariously together.
card.rules: See the Hall of the Hag Queen rulebook for details of the Bottomless Pit.

card.name: BRIDGE OF DESPAIR
card.description: This rickety bridge spans a deadly spike lined pit. Flanked by dungeon denizens intent on knocking the adventurers into the deadly defile. However the bridge itself holds further surprises yet.
card.rules: Rules can be found in the Pits and Traps Supplement.

card.name: CHASM OF DESPAIR
card.description: This narrow bridge crosses a deep chasm.
card.rules: Any Warrior or Monster that rolls a natural 1 to hit in hand-to-hand combat will lose their balance and fall to their death in the chasm below. A Warrior may only be rescued by use of a rope or the levitate spell. You must test to see if the rope breaks as normal.

card.name: CIRCLE OF POWER
card.description: Shards of flickering, unnatural green light pierce the darkness from within this room.
card.rules: The Circle of Power will always trigger an Event Card, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: COLLAPSED PASSAGE
card.description: A cave-in has nearly sealed this passageway, forcing the Warriors to walk in single file.
card.rules: This passageway will be empty unless an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase.

card.name: CORNER
card.description: The corridor suddenly and sharply twists into the darkness.
card.rules: The Corner will always be empty unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase.

card.name: CROSSROADS OF DOOM
card.description: A secret passage way? A devious trap? A dastardly ambush? A hidden treasure horde? All it takes is a pull of the lever to find out.
card.rules: Rules can be found in the Pits and Traps Supplement.

card.name: CRYSTAL CAVE
card.description: Protruding out of the cave walls are enormous crystals which seem to pulsate with energy.
card.rules: The first time a Warrior enters this Board Section they roll 1D6 and add that number to the Power Phase roll. The total is the number of Wounds they heal, up to their Starting Wounds score.

card.name: DEAD END
card.description: A rockfall blocks the tunnel ahead and there seems no obvious way deeper into the dungeon.
card.rules: See White Dwarf 192 for details of the Dead End.

card.name: DREAD KING'S THRONE ROOM
card.description: This massive chamber is the royal hall of the Liche King himself. The Warriors can see the Dread King seated upon his ornate throne.
card.rules: See the Catacombs of Terror rulebook for details of the Dread King's Throne Room.

card.name: DUNGEON CELL
card.description: What little light penetrates this room seems to be absorbed by its deep blue floor.
card.rules: The Dungeon Cell will always trigger an Event Card, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: DWARVEN FORGE
card.description: An intense light radiates from this room, you can hear the sizzle of hot lava, feel the blistering heat and smell the noxious fumes.
card.rules: See article for details of Dwarven Forge.

card.name: FIGHTING PIT
card.description: The dim light of a single lamp suspended from the ceiling barely penetrates the shadows of this dark and forbidding room.
card.rules: See the Adventure Book for details of the Fighting Pit.

card.name: FIRECHASM
card.description: A deep red light seeps out from this room, suffusing the area with a burning glow. The air is almost too hot to breathe and the walls are warm to the touch.
card.rules: See the Adventure Book for details of the Firechasm.

card.name: FLAMES OF KHAZLA
card.description: Nestled in the corner ahead is a small shrine of multi-coloured flames.
card.rules: The first time a Warrior enters this board section, he regains 2D6 Wounds, up to his starting Wounds maximum. Each Warrior may only be healed once in this way. The Flames of Khazla is a corridor, not a dungeon room, so entering it will not trigger an Event card.

card.name: FOUNTAIN OF LIGHT
card.description: A faint yellow glow emanates from this room, and you can just hear the trickle of running water.
card.rules: See the Adventure Book for details of the Fountain of Light.

card.name: GORGUT'S LAIR
card.description: This huge room has an air of menace. Facing the Warriors is a deep, spiked pit. Beyond the pit the Warriors can see a dimly-lit platform upon which stands an imposing throne.
card.rules: See the Lair of the Orc Lord rulebook for details of Gorgut's Lair.

card.name: GUARD ROOM
card.description: The dim light of your lantern illuminates what appears to be a floor covered in a checkerboard pattern.
card.rules: The Guard Room will always trigger an Event Card, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: HALL OF DEATH
card.description: This bone-filled room reeks of terror, decay and death.
card.rules: When the Warriors find this room, split the rest of the dungeon deck between the three exits, just as for a T-junction. Take three Event cards, without looking at them, and place one by each exit. When exploring these exits, as well as turning over the next Dungeon card, resolve the Event card for that doorway too.

card.name: IDOL CHAMBER
card.description: The wan light of your lantern glints off the cold stone walls of this chamber, revealing a hideous stone idol at the far end of the room.
card.rules: See the Adventure Book for details of the Idol Chamber.

card.name: INTO THE DARK
card.description: A set of ancient stairs descends into the depths of the dungeon.
card.rules: See White Dwarf 192 for details of this room.

card.name: LANDING
card.description: This shadowy corridor leads to a flight of steps.
card.rules: The Landing will always be empty unless an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase. Choose one of two doorway positions.

card.name: MONSTERS' LAIR
card.description: As you peer into the gloom, the lantern's light glints off what seems to be bones.
card.rules: The Monsters' Lair will always trigger an Event Card, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: PASSAGEWAY I
card.description: You face a moss covered corridor, its end hidden in the gloom.
card.rules: The Passageway will always be empty unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase. Choose one of three doorway positions.

card.name: PASSAGEWAY II
card.description: You are standing just inside a dank, dark expanse of corridor that disappears into the gloom.
card.rules: The Passageway will always be empty unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase. Choose one of three doorway positions.

card.name: QUIRRIK'S LABORATORY
card.description: The stench of burning warpstone fills the air, and the ominous hum of powerful machinery makes the floor shake. In the darkness you spy the bulky form of the warpfire generator!
card.rules: See White Dwarf 195 for details.

card.name: SEWER
card.description: The rancid stench of noxious vapours assails the Warrior's nostrils as they reach this intersection with an effluent channel.
card.rules: See White Dwarf 204 for details of the Sewer.

card.name: SHAMAN'S DEN
card.description: The Warriors can feel the magical power in the room, but the poor light of the lantern is not enough to show what might lurk within. Crumbling sandstone steps lead up into the darkness.
card.rules: The Shaman's Den will always trigger an Event card unless the Lair of the Orc Lord rulebook states otherwise. There are special rules for this room - see the Shaman's Den special card.

card.name: STAIRWAY
card.description: You almost stumble and fall down a set of slimy stone steps that descend into the darkness.
card.rules: The Stairway will always be empty unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase.

card.name: T-JUNCTION
card.description: The passage divides into two here, leaving you with no idea as to which direction to take.
card.rules: The T-junction will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase.

card.name: TEMPLE OF KHAINE
card.description: A towering statue of Khaine greets visitors. On a platform behind it is a cauldron dripping with blood.
card.rules: See the Hall of the Hag Queen rulebook for details about the Temple of Khaine. Choose one of two doorway positions.

card.name: THE GAOL
card.description: This tiny cell is dark and damp and smells of rotten flesh.
card.rules: See White Dwarf 185 for details of the Gaol. Draw another Objective Room card to find out your adventure.

card.name: TOMB CHAMBER
card.description: A chill breeze blows from this room. In the flickering light, you can make out the shape of a stone slab inside.
card.rules: See the Adventure Book for details of the Tomb Chamber.

card.name: TORTURE CHAMBER
card.description: By the flickering light of your lantern, you can just make out a small rough-hewn room.
card.rules: The Torture Chamber will always trigger an Event Card, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.

card.name: WATER CAVE
card.description: You see deep, murky water and are fairly certain that something just swam by.
card.rules: See the Hall of the Hag Queen rulebook for details of the Water Cave.

card.name: WELL OF DOOM
card.description: The stench of decay hangs heavy in the air, emanating from a bottomless shaft in the centre of the room.
card.rules: The Well of Doom will always trigger an Event Card, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.
